text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived high atop Oakwood Tower. Every morning she would glide gracefully southward through the dapperустuous leaves toward her favorite branch—the elm where she lounged reading aloud stories from forgotten volumes.

Once she'd arrive there snugly between the sturdy branches, she'd settle down and listen. Underneath the ancient oak would flutter busy bees busy collecting honey—but owl ears could pick up nothing of that. Instead her ears would twitch just slightly.

One morning something sounded terribly different. Trees rustled—a flock of ravens perhaps, but surely nothing villainous. Owl shifted comfortably.

A few nights later foxes came—and stealthily! They took berries. There'd been none last year.

"Oh Dear." Owl whispered.

That autumn she changed her spots nightly. Foxes spared her none.

Then one day a wolf? She watched from her high perch.

"Why?' owlThought."

Just howling. Just howling.

Could anything want an owl?

Days passed—it
